behavior_summary: U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch runs when an egg item triggers its U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch event and, under certain conditions, teleports the player/avatar to the egg while playing fade and effect animations and disabling player control during the sequence.
If Egg::getEggId(egg_item) == 1 the teleport sequence always runs; which vertical offset is used for the teleport depends on the global flag mythranMet.
If Egg::getEggId(egg_item) != 1 then the function checks Item::getZ(egg_item) and only runs the teleport sequence when the egg's Z < 0x30 (48).
The visible sequence is: setAvatarInStasis(1) → FadeToBlack(pid) → spawn FREE::29D8(effect params) → teleportToEgg(pid, x=0, y_offset, flags=0x08) → FadeFromBlack(pid) → setAvatarInStasis(0).
notable_control_flow: Branch on Egg::getEggId(egg_item) == 1 to select unconditional-teleport path vs. height-guarded path.
In the Egg-id==1 branch an additional read of global mythranMet chooses one of two Y-offsets (short or long) for teleportToEgg.
In the non-id-1 branch the teleport only proceeds if Item::getZ(egg_item) < 0x30 (48); otherwise the function does nothing visible.
Process calls (FadeToBlack, teleportToEgg, FadeFromBlack) are invoked and awaited/synchronized in order; the avatar is put into stasis for the duration.
open_questions: Exact gameplay meaning of the numeric teleport parameters (the three observed Y-offsets 0x04, 0x14, 0x16) and how they map to in-world positions.
Precise visual/audio content and gameplay effect of the spawned FREE::29D8 effect (parameters observed but semantic meaning is inferred).
Whether the U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch event is always triggered by direct player interaction or can be triggered by other actors/processes in some contexts.
Purpose of the init_local_0 local (initialized but unused in the excerpt) — is it a required ABI/init slot or reserved for future logic?

main_function.name: U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch
main_function.purpose: Coordinate a guarded teleport/fade/effect sequence to move the avatar to an egg when that egg 'hatches' or is activated, temporarily disabling avatar control during the sequence.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the egg item's Event 7 (U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch).
Runs whenever the U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch event occurs, but the actual teleport may be gated by egg type (Egg::getEggId) or egg height (Item::getZ).

function_map.name: U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch
function_map.role: Orchestrates the U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch teleport/fade/effect flow and reads condition flags/state from the egg and a global flag.

external_calls.purpose: Read the egg's type/id to decide which branch of the U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch logic to take (id==1 forces teleport sequence).
Read the egg's Z (height) to gate the teleport sequence in the non-id-1 branch (only proceed if Z < 0x30).
Disable avatar control at the start of the teleport/fade sequence (called with 1) and re-enable afterwards (called with 0).
Fade the player's screen to black and synchronize before spawning effects/teleporting the avatar.
Spawn a transient effect during the U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch/teleport sequence (observed params: 0x64, 0x07, 0x00000000); likely a visual or audio flourish associated with hatching.
Teleport the specified pid to the egg's location using a small x-byte (often 0) and a branch-dependent Y-offset and flags (0x08).
Fade the player's screen back in after the teleport completes and synchronize before restoring control.

reads.name: mythranMet
reads.likely_effect: Selects which Y-offset teleportToEgg uses when Egg::getEggId(egg_item) == 1 (affects final avatar placement).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch runs when an egg item triggers its U8P_MYTHAUS::hatch event and, under certain conditions, teleports the player/avatar to the egg while playing fade and effect animations and disabling player control during the sequence.
